Volkswagen Says It Has Enough Chips For Production Next Week, But Disruption Can't Be Ruled Out
data.description:
By Dominic Chopping


Volkswagen has enough chips to continue manufacturing through next week, but continued to caution that short-term impacts to its group production network can't be ruled out, a spokesperson said Friday.

Global automakers are scrambling to secure supplies of chips after the Dutch government last month seized control of semiconductor manufacturer Nexperia from its Chinese owner Wingtech Technology, citing risks to economic security.

China retaliated by halting exports of certain Nexperia products manufactured in China, prompting carmakers to start searching for alternative products that are needed to run things such as electronic control units.

Volkswagen earlier this week said that production wasn't affected, but that short-term effects on production couldn't be ruled out.

In an update Friday, a spokesperson said the supply shortage is having no impact on production at the vehicle manufacturing plants of the Volkswagen brand in Germany for the time being.

At all other German production sites that manufacture cars under the other brands within the group, operations for the coming week are also secured as things stand Friday. As well as its namesake brand, Volkswagen also manufactures Audi and Porsche cars in Germany, among others.

"In view of the dynamic situation, however, short-term impacts on the production network of the Volkswagen Group cannot generally be ruled out," the spokesperson said.

Vehicle production at its Wolfsburg, Emden, Zwickau, Osnabruck, and Dresden sites is secured for the coming week, up to and including next Thursday. Production will be suspended next Friday due to public holidays.

Nexperia isn't a direct supplier to the group, although some Nexperia parts are used in vehicle components supplied by the carmaker's vendors.

Volkswagen is looking at alternative sourcing options to limit the possible effects on its supply chain and is also in close contact with potential suppliers, the spokesperson added.


Write to Dominic Chopping at dominic.chopping@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-24-25 0921ET

China Coal Xinji Energy Co.,Ltd Reports Earnings Results for the Nine Months Ended September 30, 2025
data.description:
China Coal Xinji Energy Co.,Ltd reported earnings results for the nine months ended September 30, 2025. For the nine months, the company reported sales was CNY 9,009.71 million compared to CNY 9,189.12 million a year ago. Revenue was CNY 9,009.71 million compared to CNY 9,189.12 million a year ago.

Net income was CNY 1,476.57 million compared to CNY 1,824.32 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was CNY 0.57 compared to CNY 0.7 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was CNY 0.57 compared to CNY 0.7 a year ago.

Illinois Tool Works tightens annual profit forecast on tariff headwinds
data.description:
(Reuters) -Illinois Tool Works narrowed its annual profit forecast range on Friday, as the industrial parts maker expects supply chain snarls due to U.S. tariffs.

Shares of the company fell nearly 4% in premarket trading.

U.S. President Donald Trump's tariff policies have driven up prices of raw materials, forcing companies such as Illinois Tool Works to raise prices and reshore supply chains to preserve margins.

The company now expects 2025 profit between $10.40 and $10.50 per share, compared with its prior projection of $10.35 to $10.55 per share.

But it posted a third-quarter profit of $2.81 per share, beating analysts' estimates of $2.71 per share, according to data compiled by LSEG.

The automotive OEM segment was the biggest contributor to overall revenue with $830 million, compared with $772 million a year ago.

The company supplies components and fasteners to automakers including Ford and BMW.

Its overall quarterly revenue rose 2.3% to $4.06 billion, missing analysts' average estimate of $4.08 billion.

(Reporting by Parth Chandna; Editing by Sahal Muhammed)

data.title: The Walt Disney Company Appoints Rochelle Knock as Country Head, Sub-Saharan Africa, Effective December
data.description:
The Walt Disney Company announced the appointment of Rochelle Knock as Country Head, sub-Saharan Africa, effective December. Knock joins the entertainment giant from Yoco Technologies, where she most recently served as Head of Customer Value Management. In her new role, Knock will lead Disney's strategic vision and operations across sub-Saharan Africa, driving business growth and fostering collaboration across teams to deliver on new opportunities in the region.

She will oversee the company's direct-to-consumer segment, including Disney+, as well as its family and factual linear networks. Additionally, she will partner with the platform distribution team and support broader initiatives led at the regional EMEA level, ensuring that Disney's local operations align with the company's global brand, franchise and business priorities.

data.title: Johnson & Johnson Announces New Long-Term Results from the Phase 3 ICONIC-TOTAL Studya Evaluating icotrokinra
data.description:
Johnson & Johnson announced new long-term 52-week data from the Phase 3 ICONIC-TOTAL studya evaluating icotrokinra, a first-in-class investigational targeted oral peptide that precisely blocks the IL-23 receptor, in adults and pediatric patients 12 years of age and older (adolescents) with plaque psoriasis (PsO) affecting high-impact sites. The ICONIC-TOTAL study, presented at the 2025 Fall Clinical Dermatology Conference, simultaneously evaluated adults and adolescents with at least moderate scalp, genital and/or hand/foot plaque psoriasis with =1% Body Surface Area (BSA) affected. Through Week 52, icotrokinra demonstrated high and durable rates of site-specific psoriasis clearance affecting all of these high-impact and difficult-to-treat areas of the body.

In the smaller subset of patients with hand/foot psoriasis, treatment with icotrokinra showed a numerically higher rate of skin clearance at Week 16, which increased through Week 52 with patients achieving a hand and/or foot Physician's Global Assessment (hf-PGA)e score of 0/1 increasing from 42% to 62%. Overall response rates among patients treated with once daily icotrokinra were maintained through Week 52, with 67% of patients treated with icotrokinra achieving clear or almost clear skin (Investigator's Global Assessment (IGA)f 0/1) and 44% achieving completely clear skin (IGA 0) at Week 52. The overall response rates were also comparable among patients who received icotrokinra for all 52 weeks and those who transitioned from placebo to icotrokinra at Week 16 (67% versus 68% achieved IGA 0/1, respectively). Across treatment groups, adverse event and serious adverse event rates were similar through Week 52 compared to those through Week 16, with no new safety signals identified.

data.title: Eli Lilly Agrees to Acquire Adverum Biotechnologies
data.description:
By Adriano Marchese


Eli Lilly said Friday it is set to acquire clinical-stage company Adverum Biotechnologies for up to $12.47 a share, including milestone payments.

Under the terms of the agreement, Lilly will launch a tender offer to acquire all of the outstanding shares of Adverum common stock for a per-share price of $3.56 in cash as well as one contingent value right that entitles holders to receive up to an additional $8.91 per CVR in cash upon the achievement of certain milestones.

Adverum is developing a pipeline of gene therapies as it aims to find functional cures to restore vision and prevent blindness. Its lead product candidate, Ixo-vec, a treatment of wet age-related macular degeneration, a chronic and progressive retinal disease.

Shares of Adverum have been under pressure in 2025, falling about 10% to $4.18 since the year began. The stock is down 48% over the last 52 weeks. Shares are halted from trading on Friday in premarket.

Ixo-vec is currently in a phase 3 clinical trial called Artemis and has been granted fast-track and regenerative medicine advanced therapy designations by the Food and Drug Administration. It also has special designations in Europe and the U.K.


Write to Adriano Marchese at adriano.marchese@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-24-25 0904ET

data.title: Dr Reddy's exec says not looking for partnership with Eli Lilly for launching Tirzepatide in India
Sika: stock flattish after slight decline in 9m results
data.description:
Sika shares are fairly stable in Zurich today, after the chemical group announced 9m 2025 EBITDA of CHF 1.64bn, down 3.3% due to significant currency effects, albeit representing a 0.1-point increase in its margin to 19.2%.

At CHF 8.58bn, its sales declined by 3.8%, weighed on by currency fluctuations (mainly the depreciation of the dollar), without which they increased by 1.1% in local currency, despite a double-digit decline in construction activity in China.

Even in an environment marked by strong headwinds, we continued to gain market share. We are actively addressing the persistent weakness in the markets through structural adjustments, it said.

For 2025, Sika confirms that it expects a slight increase in sales in local currency, despite a shrinking global market and difficult conditions in China. Its EBITDA margin is expected to be around 19% (between 19.5% and 19.8% excluding non-recurring costs).

data.title: Uber, WeRide Launch Robotaxi Service in Saudi Arabia
data.description:
By Connor Hart


Uber Technologies and WeRide have partnered to launch robotaxi rides in Riyadh, marking the first time autonomous vehicles have been publicly available on Uber's platform in Saudi Arabia.

Each vehicle includes a safety operator for now, a measure the companies said Friday will help ensure reliability and safety for riders and pedestrians. They plan to move to fully driverless operations in the future, though no timeline was given.

The launch follows Uber's collaboration with Saudi Arabia's Transport General Authority and is part of the country's push to expand autonomous mobility. The service will initially operate between Roshn Front and Princess Noura University, two major destinations in the capital.

The ride-hailing company has been working with WeRide and regional authorities to expand access to autonomous vehicles across the Middle East.

It previously began offering WeRide robotaxi rides in Abu Dhabi late last year.


Write to Connor Hart at connor.hart@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-24-25 0903ET
